name: Collado de Cherre — circular by the Sierra de las Pedrosas
name_original: Collado de Cherre — circular por la Sierra de las Pedrosas
region_intro: Hiking around Las Pedrosas offers diverse landscapes, from distinctive steppe scenery to distant mountainous vistas. The region provides panoramic views stretching across plains and towards the Pyrenees. A notable feature is the Sierra de las Pedrosas, which includes ecologically significant landscapes with unique geological characteristics. The area combines open plains with views of pine forests, providing varied terrain for exploration. Best hiking trails around Las Pedrosas The most popular hiking route is Aguarales de Valpalmas from Piedratajada, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete. This moderate route features varied terrain with gentle elevation changes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Collado de Cherre — circular by the Sierra de las Pedrosas, a moderate 5.6 miles (9.0 km) path. This circular trail explores the northern slope of the Sierra de las Pedrosas, offering scenic views. Local hikers also love the Cueva de Carlos loop from Castejón de Valdejasa, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 21 minutes. Hiking around Las Pedrosas is defined by its steppe landscape, panoramic views, and the Sierra de las Pedrosas. The network offers options for different ability levels, primarily moderate trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 8 hikers have used komoot to explore Las Pedrosas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Las Pedrosas?
A: Las Pedrosas is characterized by its distinctive steppe landscape, offering a unique environment for exploration. You'll also encounter panoramic views stretching from the plains of La Violada to the dramatic Mallos de Riglos, and distant vistas of the Pyrenees. The area includes the Sierra de las Pedrosas, which features ecologically significant landscapes with special geological characteristics.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Las Pedrosas?
A: Yes, a prominent feature for outdoor activities is the circular route around the municipality. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981894150/collado-de-cherre-circular-by-the-sierra-de-las-pedrosas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de Cherre — circular by the Sierra de las Pedrosas&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.6-mile (9.0 km) path that explores the northern slope of the Sierra, offering scenic views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38161480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Carlos loop from Castejón de Valdejasa&lt;/a&gt;, a 7.4-mile (11.9 km) trail through varied landscapes.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for hiking trails in Las Pedrosas?
A: The hiking network around Las Pedrosas primarily offers moderate trails. There are no easy or difficult routes listed in the current guide, making it suitable for hikers with some experience looking for engaging, yet manageable, walks.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can discover on hikes in the area?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore historical and natural landmarks. Highlights include the historic church of Santa María la Mayor, ancient 16th and 17th-century houses, and unique monuments like the Pozo de Hielo (Ice Well). For scenic views, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312160" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of La Mula&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1697417" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de San Esteban&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3716853" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Monlora and Viewpoint of the Cinco Villas&lt;/a&gt; also offers impressive vistas.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Las Pedrosas?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not provided, the moderate difficulty of the trails suggests that some routes could be suitable for families with older children or those accustomed to hiking. The varied terrain and historical points of interest can make for an engaging outing. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e947206102/aguarales-de-valpalmas-from-piedratajada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguarales de Valpalmas from Piedratajada&lt;/a&gt; is a 6.0-mile (9.6 km) moderate trail with varied terrain and gentle elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Las Pedrosas?
A: The region's steppe landscape and open vistas suggest that spring and autumn would be ideal for hiking, offering milder temperatures and pleasant conditions for enjoying the panoramic views. Summers might be hot due to the open terrain, while winters could be chilly but offer a different perspective of the landscape.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Las Pedrosas?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on trails are not detailed, many natural areas in Spain are dog-friendly, especially on established paths. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and ensure they are well-behaved to respect wildlife and other hikers. The tranquil atmosphere of Las Pedrosas could be enjoyable for a hike with your canine companion.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of hikes in Las Pedrosas?
A: The hikes in Las Pedrosas generally range from moderate distances. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e947206102/aguarales-de-valpalmas-from-piedratajada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguarales de Valpalmas from Piedratajada&lt;/a&gt; is about 6.0 miles (9.6 km) and takes around 2 hours 33 minutes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981894150/collado-de-cherre-circular-by-the-sierra-de-las-pedrosas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de Cherre — circular by the Sierra de las Pedrosas&lt;/a&gt; is 5.6 miles (9.0 km) and takes approximately 2 hours 30 minutes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38161480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Carlos loop from Castejón de Valdejasa&lt;/a&gt; is longer at 7.4 miles (11.9 km), typically completed in about 3 hours 21 minutes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Las Pedrosas?
A: Hikers appreciate the diverse landscapes, from the unique steppe scenery to the distant mountainous vistas. The panoramic views, especially those stretching towards the Pyrenees and the Mallos de Riglos, are frequently highlighted. The tranquility and the opportunity to explore ecologically significant areas like the Sierra de las Pedrosas also contribute to a rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the hiking trails in Las Pedrosas?
A: Las Pedrosas offers a peaceful atmosphere and the friendliness of its inhabitants, suggesting local amenities. While specific cafes or accommodations directly on trails are not detailed, the town itself, being just 30 minutes from Zaragoza, would likely provide options for refreshments and lodging, enhancing a relaxing natural getaway.

Q: What kind of wildlife or ecological features can be observed in the area?
A: The Sierra de las Pedrosas is noted for its 'ecologically significant landscapes with special geological and topographical characteristics.' While specific flora and fauna are not extensively detailed, the emphasis on its ecological value suggests a rich natural environment. Hikers can expect to observe the unique adaptations of life to the steppe environment and potentially various bird species.
tips: We are near the Collado de Cherre since we have gone to the highest point of the Sierra de las Pedrosas at 761 meters of altitude along a path from the Collado that gives us beautiful views of the Sierra de Guara, the Sierra de Santo Domingo, the Pyrenees, Monlora, the pine forests of Castejón and Zuera, Moncayo and the surrounding towns
